title: Lekki Deep Seaport gains ground as Apapa Port market share drops
description:
Lekki Deep Sea Port has continued to chip into Apapa Port’s dominance in Nigeria’s maritime trade, as new data shows a gradual redistribution of cargo volumes across the country’s major seaports.

The post Lekki Deep Seaport gains ground as Apapa Port market share drops  appeared first on Nairametrics.
content:
Lekki Deep Sea Port has continued to chip into Apapa Port’s dominance in Nigeria’s maritime trade, as new data shows a gradual redistribution of cargo volumes across the country’s major seaports.

The post Lekki Deep Seaport gains ground as Apapa Port market share drops  appeared first on Nairametrics.
summary: The Lekki Deep Seaport's growing market share at the expense of Apapa Port is a significant development for Lagos real estate, as it signals increased economic activity and infrastructure investment in the region, potentially driving demand for premium and commercial properties.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This development may positively impact Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe, as increased economic activity in the Lekki area could drive demand for premium properties.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The growing Lekki Deep Seaport may also lead to increased costs and regulatory pressures, affecting the pricing and costs of Mixta Africa's projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The Lekki Deep Seaport's growth presents a strategic opportunity for Mixta Africa to capitalize on the increasing demand for premium and commercial properties in the region.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: However, Mixta Africa should closely monitor the regulatory environment and potential changes in the market, as the growing Lekki Deep Seaport may also pose risks to the company's execution and profitability.

title: Why Lagos corporates are ditching fleet ownership for car rentals – Muvment CEO
